plugin_desc: Sugon storage-specific SNMP collection for Sugon ParaStor/storage arrays (IANA PEN 35904). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Sugon private OIDs for device health — per-fan status (fanState), per-power-supply status (powerState) and per-disk health status (diskState). The fan, power-supply and disk-health raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault and the worst component is taken per device with max(). The Sugon array exposes hardware component status under the private tree but does not publish a pollable controller-wide CPU or memory utilization gauge, so CPU and memory utilization are not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — Sugon ParaStor and DS-series storage arrays with SNMP enabled.
name: Storage

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan health of the Sugon storage array, derived from the per-fan status table (fanState, one row per fan). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other state is treated as a fault, and the worst fan is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a failed fan that risks overheating the controller enclosure.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the Sugon storage array, derived from the per-power-supply status table (powerState, one row per power supply). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other state is treated as a fault, and the worst supply is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a power-supply fault or loss of power redundancy.

metrics.name: device_disk_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the physical disk health of the Sugon storage array, derived from the per-disk status table (diskState, one row per disk). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other state is treated as a fault, and the worst disk is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a degraded or failed disk that risks data loss and warrants immediate replacement.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed storage uplinks, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed storage uplinks, keeping the rate accurate.
